reviews.title: OK for the price
reviews.text: Bought this speaker to improve podcasts on my tablet. Improvement over tablet speakers for sure but no comparison to $150 Bose Soundlink Mini II as you would expect. Music sounds OK for background but not much bass. Pairs pretty easily to Bluetooth. Occasionally when I leave it on when I'm done listening it won't automatically shut down and I get some random noises later when I'm in a different room. Overall I'd say it's worth the price I paid on sale but don't expect high fidelity.

reviews.author: ICON

reviews.title: Wanted to love this... TERRIBLE choice for an offi
reviews.text:
Pros: compact, cute, was 60% off
Cons: 
1) Volume control is *mute, *quiet, *really loud, or *are you kidding me with this I'm RIGHT here!... there is no way to adjust up/down a little, each click is a huge jump.
2) When you turn it on it screams at you "powering on, welcome to altec lansing"... unnecessary, unavoidable, and WAY too loud
3) The aux cord, should you want to connect to a desktop (such as my work computer) fits poorly... it stays in because gravity doesn't fight it, but it's loose enough you can easily knock it out of the jack
4) When you power it on, after it's done yelling at you, it tries to Bluetooth connect to a device. If you do not pair it, for instance if you want to use the aux cord, it will try to pair for 10 minutes, beep at you because you failed to do what it wanted you to do, then power off.

I'm assuming there's someone out there that these features work for, but if you want this for your desk at work, if you want any sensitive control over volume, or if you want to (gasp!) actually USE the ancient tech of an aux cord... get something else. Even at 60% off, it's not worth it. I'm returning it today.

reviews.author: Randy

reviews.title: Really Good Deal of the Day purchase
reviews.text:
I got this for 39.00 on the Deal of the Day. Altec Lansing is a company I have trusted for many years. At the $99.00 MSRP it has really strong competition from UE, Bose and others but nothing competes with it at the sale price.

It makes for very good but not great music, is waterproof (but not submergible!) and plays plenty loud when close at hand outdoors and more than loud enough in a small room where I use it. It has decent bass and for those who are into heavy bass you may want something else. I like acoustic music- Jazz, vocal, folk, bluegrass and it is really good for these.
